commit cbce755ba20d0e940698d02a9eeb1e775d22300e
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-14 01:04:24 +1000

Increased the min elapsed turns for the circumnavigation bonus.

The circumnavigation bonus is only granted if the circumnavigation does
not occur on the first turn of the game. This is so that games which
start with a complete map don't give an unfair advantage to player #1.

Since CvTeam::doTurn is now called after the game turn is incremented,
the circumnatigation min-turn needed to be increased from > 0 to > 1.

commit cdc39b892959f9601fbf4f502ab379188099f107
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-13 21:18:10 +1000

ctrl/alt select will no long ignore moves when unit is already selected.

When selecting a group of units by holding ctrl/alt, if the clicked unit
is already selected then only units with moves will be selected in the
new group.

This new condition is because we can't tell the difference between a
click on the map and a click on the unit icon; and because of this, the
'ignore moves' rule can make it a bit difficult to select moveable units
when a unit without moves is already selected.

commit 821d295fd113dbc6d68629aaf0274e1cc11e0193
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-13 21:14:34 +1000

AI_assignWorkingPlots now clears specialists in some situations.

Specialists will once again be cleared and reassigned for automated city
with forced specialists. Without this, the loose rule that all
specialists should be the same type as the forced specialist breaks
down.

commit 5ed70f2e640a498faec0c50753ddc88b5167291d
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-07 19:06:42 +1000

Tweaked the sort order in the revised AI_findXRank functions.

Changed the sorting from (score, cityID) in decending order, to
(-score, cityID) in ascending order; the affect of this change is that
lower cityIDs will be ranked higher, which is consistent with the
original code, and is more intuitive in certain in-game situations.

commit 233dc3b73fb30d17ef8b096a84cc94742162edb4
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-05 22:16:59 +1000

Added 'high food' check to bonuses added by normalizeAddFoodBonuses

Although existing land food bonuses which only net 3 food are only
counted for 2 points; bonuses which were added to boost the total score
were previously not subject to this test... This means it was still
possible to have a low food capital city in some rare situations.

This is now fixed.

commit 8be9088d15ac9f4004a245ad580463a49ba2958e
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-05 17:24:28 +1000

Rewrote CvCityAI::AI_getYieldMultipliers; fixed a food scaling problem.

The new version of AI_getYieldMultipliers is similar to old, but it is a
bit simplier, it includes a few flavour adjustments; but most
importantly, the food value multiplier now scales up as other yields
become more valued. This is to avoid the problem where production can
end up being worth more than the food that is necessary to work the
production.

Some other related CvCityAI functions have been tweaked to be consistent
with the new version of AI_getYieldMultipliers.

commit 26754a44823d8188aa91e3f3053b6f2baab7fe19
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-05 16:41:03 +1000

The CvCity::findXRank functions now update rank for all cities at once.

This includes findPopulationRank, findBaseYieldRateRank,
findYieldRateRank, and findCommerceRateRank.

In the original BtS code, these functions would compare the current city
to every other city to find its rank, and thus only set the cache for
the city being queried. The new code gathers all of the city data, sorts
it, and set the rank cache for every city in one pass.

commit 7afc7dc16f392cf706a8825c0dd67dbf21ccd6fe
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-05 14:51:34 +1000

Fixed a possible crash in AI_warCommitmentCost and changed overseas cost

AI_warCommitmentCost would previously divide by zero when evaluating an
overseas war in which neither was able to build any boats. This has been
corrected.

Also, the flat adjustement of 5/4 for total war overseas has been
replaced by a factor which depends on the relative strength of the
ground forces.

commit 354b621fa170a8c28448826f6be6503fffb82162
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-05 13:44:39 +1000

AI_getYieldWithBuild now counts yield from existing improvements.

Previously AI_getYieldWithBuild would only count yield from improvements
created by the build itself. And so, for example, if you were building a
railroad on a mine, the functino would count the natural yield of the
plot, and the extra yield from the railroad, but not from the mine
itself.

This is now corrected.

commit c8eebd28a6f52c6a924d4459a6cd6d74b882b7d9

Renamed AI_getGoodTileCount and AI_countWorkedPoorTiles

CvCityAI::AI_getGoodTileCount renamed to AI_countGoodPlots.
CvCityAI::AI_countWorkedPoorTiles renamed to AI_countWorkedPoorPlots.

(The terminology of "plots" and "count" is more consistent with the rest
of the code.)

commit 825f5cc7bf05cab24b4a90e406e2e03dff4bc52f
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-05 13:21:10 +1000

Added CvCityAI::AI_isGoodPlot, and used this to removed duplicated code.

The bulk of AI_getGoodTileCount and AI_countWorkedPoorTiles has been
replaced by calls to CvCityAI::AI_isGoodPlot.

Note: the 'good plot' check in AI_getYieldMultipliers hasn't yet been
replaced; because that function is about to be completely restructured.

commit 5c447069c348b95a1604992cc6376388f6f6355f
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-04 12:25:39 +1000

Made some stop-gap adjustments to the AI's improvement yield evaluations

There are some serious flaws in the AI's evaluation of yield when
choosing which improvements to build. In particular, the value of food
does not scale when the value of jobs that could be worked with more
food; and the value of yield in general is inconistent with the way the
AI chooses which plots to work.. And so sometimes the AI will replace
good improvements with new ones which won't be worked; or replace high
food improvements with other improvements which won't have enough food
to be worked...

I don't address the core of these problem in this minor update, but I
have made a few changes with might help. In particular, I've decreased
the multiplier used for production, so that production doesn't end up
being worth signficantly more than food.

commit 1db9e9e05f82c6ae55ba21505ce8da8379874d53

Adjusted the conditions and effect of bChop in AI_updateBestBuild

bChop will now be set true for pretty much everything when the civ has
less than the target number of cities. Also, the priority of best build
is now increased when it produces chopping yield when bChop is true.

commit baa112cd9d2d031e765d1db6d0c7f698a25d1d11

left of center.

commit 322e8c9da1c997a57780bfe21043ed5351579814
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-03 13:58:21 +1000

Added function AI_getAvailableIncome. Replaced old code for consistency.

AI_updateAvailableIncome calculates and caches an estimate of the total
gold-per-turn revenue the player could achieve if they used 100% gold on
the commerce slider. This function is called once per turn, and the
result is accessed with AI_getAvailableIncome.

AI_getAvailableIncome is now used to replace some previous income
estimates which typically worked by simply adding
getCommerceRate(COMMERCE_SCIENCE) + getCommerceRate(COMMERCE_GOLD) + std::max(0, goldPerTurn())
The old calculation was flawed in that it failed to correctly handle
espionage or culture on the commerce slider.

commit b8f96f9b4611e08b6979859811e36959bc66cea4
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-03 13:46:29 +1000

Fixed a misuse of getGoldPerTurn in CvPlayerAI::AI_commerceWeight.

CvPlayer::getGoldPerTurn refers only to gold from trade deals. The value
that AI_commerceWeight really wanted was calculateGoldRate.
(Unfortunately, the corrected version is slightly slower. - But at least
it makes sense!)

commit 51365d78de5376add46ebe20b1848b1098d3cad4

commit a3f99f4addd7eefaf46652089cbea056ecd1a847
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-02 14:07:53 +1000

Made some AI changes in CvCityAI::AI_updateSpecialYieldMultiplier.

The adjustments associated with workboats, workers, and settlers are now
all the same. The effect of building a workboat is now less significant.

GetBetterUnit and EconomyFocus now result in a boost to commerce value.
The 'production' strategy now boosts production and lowers commerce.

Reduced the magnitude of the commerce value reduction when at war; and
made it more conditional. Also, increased the productivity value boost
for war when using dagger or crush.

commit f27e17cfd663087d21309bd4bf0e9029e3d840d3
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-01 19:51:08 +1000

Made some adjustments to the AI's decision making for chopping forests.

The yield produced from chopping is no longer counted in the evaluation
of improvements. This allows us to decide whether or not the new
improvement would be better regardless of potential immediate benefits.

The bChop checks will now be bypassed if the city is building a process.

The value of chopping is now scaled up a bit if the player hasn't yet
reached the target number of cities for the map size.

commit 80de8b79849beacf49b804f2b1ee5efd33344b27
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-08-01 19:42:11 +1000

Made some change to how city names are choosen.

When a new city is created, and a name is being chosen from the
civilization's city name list, the search will now skip a name of names
corresponding to the number of cities the player has already founded.
This means that if a player renames a city that they have founded, the
game won't immediately suggest the same previous name for the next city.

Note 1: this only applies to names taken from the civilization city names
list. It does not apply to names on the player-specific city list which
may be used by some mods.
Note 2: previously used names will again
be suggested if the civ's city name list is otherwise exhausted.

Also, if the civilization's list of city names is exhausted, then the
game will suggest a _random_ name from a random civ; rather than the
first available city name from a random civ.

commit 716c35464538de357c1e115a7a1a94f318fddb7d

Fixed the domain type in CvTeam::getTypicalUnitValue.

CvTeam::getTypicalUnitValue wasn't passing eDomain to CvPlayer.
This means the domain type was basically being ignored.

commit 5cceade9c2f57524dceb05b39c8136ad6114f9f5

commit 49b250e819f326a633a9bb12be5f4be8f1978082
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-29 12:19:57 +1000

Added an avenue for the AI to use to free itself as a vassal.

CvPlayerAI::AI_considerOffer now checks the for denial of existing
vassal deals and returns 'false' if the deal would be denied.

Before this change, AI vassals would never free themselves!

(Note: on closer examination, in the original BtS code, vassals could
free themselves from AI masters but not from human masters, because of
an inconsistence in the way existing deals were being checked. So when I
changed the code to be consistent, I had unintentially taken away the
AI's avenue for canceling vassal deals with anyone at all. Now it is
fixed and better than ever.)

Also, when considering capitualion, the AI will no longer be quite so
afraid of the new master's other enemies. (ie. if the would-be master is
much stronger than the other teams they are at war with, then the
would-be vassal will not use 'we're afraid of your enemies' as an excuse
to refuse capituation.)

commit 56f8bb89cc00ae5d07dd753b668ba048262d6c85

commit 04bf601f97534921dc5573335027c7e0d9c6a467
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-28 10:04:59 +1000

Removed BBAI function CvTeam::getCurrentMasterPower. Tidied some code.

CvTeam::getCurrentMasterPower was typcially used like this:
    int iPower = isAVassal() ? getCurrentMasterPower() : getPower();
This is cumbersome, and ineffecient because it has to do a vassal check
on all teams for isAVassal, and for getCurrentMasterPower.

The following code gives the same result:
    int iPower = GET_TEAM(getMasterTeam()).getPower();

Also, there could be some confusion between 'getCurrentMasterPower'
and 'getMasterPower', which are completely different functions with
completely different purposes.

So I've deleted CvTeam::getCurrentMasterPower.

commit 240ffb6675482d970e7f66dd9b3d66fbcc26d42b
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-27 12:43:38 +1000

When joining a group, the group's mission queue is once again cleared.

I've restored this behaviour because it's otherwise difficult to prevent
the group from executing its queued missions when units are added to the
group with the shift key - because the exe tells the game to execute
automoves when shift is released.

commit 98226ebc1d782a7fe1864f775cd7c59ddfbf1ab8

commit d406f6c64a8e185979578e528fedbddccb647ed4
Author: karadoc <hidden>

Made some corrections and adjustments to CvCityAI::AI_yieldValue

Extra points for productivity when below par are now only counted if the
player doesn't have any hurry types enabled.

When estimating how much unhappiness will wear-off before the city
grows, the estimated time for growth is now more 'optimistic' (which
means the growth time is shorter, which means the unhappiness is
greater...)

current city as having 0 GPP per turn, which completely messed up the
scaling relative to the 'best' city.

The special yield value modifier effect in AI_permanentSpecialistValue
has been diluted, because that modifier is intended as more of a
short-term thing.

commit 0c0e0e98df0c28d8b8a5c186cada8a20dbd05bbb
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-25 10:28:57 +1000

Added a sytem to stop the AI from changing target cities too often.

New data: CvPlayerAI::m_iCityTargetTimer.
New functions: AI_getCityTargetTimer, AI_setCityTargetTimer.

When the AI is requested to target a city, the timer is set to 10 turns.
When the city targets are chosen normally, the timer is set to 4 turns.

Cities targets are still updated at random times, but the random updates
are blocked if the target timer is non-zero.

commit 56ecb35ec3e02ef081d1900989e3b2960204e930
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-24 21:26:15 +1000

Put new conditions on who the AI will accept city target requests from.

AI players will now only accept city target suggestions if they are
either pleased towards the asking player, or on the same team, or a
vassal of the asking player's team.

commit 3b31377843a5c0c9db1303645adaba9dc1edf2f7

Removed bogus cities from the 'why don't you attack' diplomacy screen.

screen would list cities belonging to all teams the human is at war with
regardless of who the AI is at war with.

commit 99d7a052f1e2ecdc47b18c7f55957002d05175bd
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-24 15:57:57 +1000

Fixed an flag update problem when switching in and out of debug mode.

The unit flags on non-visible plots were not being updated when
switching in out out of debug mode. When debug mode was turned on, there
were missing flags; when debug mode was turned off, there were lingering
flags.

This bug was a side-effect of my changes to CvMap::updateCenterUnit.

The 'debug' center unit now determines the flag.

commit 6c3c81c286d238c16199ceb85dc1e3192b28a0c6

was being checked; and it would bypass the headquarters check if no
particular city was being checked.

This fixes a bug which allowed the 'corporate expansion' quest and the
'hostile takeover' quest to be assigned to players who do not control
the headquarters.

commit 3ae8208f384bfbec6420efabc3ca11ce9ae11d98
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-19 14:23:09 +1000

Made some adjustments to AI_warCommitmentCost for complex war situations

Reduced the power estimate for secondary targets in offense wars. (eg.
teams brought in by defensive pacts now only have part of their power
counted.) Productivity estimates now include vassals of both teams, and
productivity of other enemies. (Hopefully this doesn't make it
significantly slower.)

commit f0524f55aed16ad27a2a2b18c73da176646cce28
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-19 14:14:27 +1000

Many bug fixes and consistency fixes related to vassals and war.

Added new function, CvTeam::getMasterTeam. For vassals, this returns the
master's team id; otherwise it returns the team's own ID.

Fixed a problem in AI_warCommitmentCost, AI_startWarVal, and
getDefensivePower, which caused defensive pacts with the target team's
master to be ignored.

Corrected some denial types in AI_vassalTrade. It no longer checks for
PEACE_NOT_POSSIBLE_US; and instead checks for WAR_NOT_POSSIBLE_US. Also,
it will now return ATTITUDE_THEM if the would-be master would refuse to
plan war, or be bought into a war, against teams that the would-be
vassal is at war with.

Restored the check for PEACE_NOT_POSSIBLE_US in AI_surrenderTrade, with
the condition that it is not checked for peace-vassal deals.

When considering starting a war with a vassal, the AI will now use the
maximum of AI_noWarAttitudeProb of the target and of the master.
(Previously it only used the target's.)

AI_warCommitmentCost now uses the target team's master for most of the
calculations, including best units, productivity estimates. Also, it now
counts war weariness due to vassals and siblings of the target, as well
as from the target itself.

commit 563dbd61f37c62b238549f670ebdceb77a9b4537
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-15 13:48:52 +1000

Slightly amplified the effect scaling in AI_warCommitmentCost.

iCommitmentPerMil is now adjusted by an absolute value at the end of the
calculation. The effect of this is that the adjustments to
iCommitmentPerMil now have relatively stronger effects. (eg. relative
power, relative production, and so on, now count for a bit more than
before.) -- Also, the standard iCommitmentPerMil has been reduced.

commit 7d5b230567f9a82283ad2650e1f1e0a49f5dccf6
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-15 13:34:55 +1000

Fortify, sentry, heal, and sea-patrol no longer block each other.

Previously all these function returned false if the unit was 'waiting'.
Now they only check that the unit isn't already doing the particular
task. eg. canSentry now returns true if the unit is fortified, but false
if the unit is already ... sentrying. (Previously it would return false
for both.)

commit bc00e144af6b2cf937eee0a7dea246907c048652

Told the AI to explicitly wake their group in a few places.

commit 26b0df8e6bdb625c63bdb662b7d051dda714d344
Author: karadoc <hidden>

commit 8fa5349bc0d3bcb4bb0c756aa9e7c9fbdfa1f303
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-13 18:06:56 +1000

startMission now deletes dud missions without changing activity.

CvSelectionGroup::startMission use to set the group's activity to either
'hold' or 'mission' before checking whether the mission was possible. It
now checks the mission first, and if canStartMission returns false, the
mission is deleted without the group's activity being changed.

With this change, fortified AI groups will no longer refortify if the
fortify mission is pushed again.

(Note: unfortunately it also means that fortified AI groups will ignore
other 'wait' missions as well, including heal and sentry. But I don't
think that will make any difference for the time being.)

commit 5348a1d503b3df09f7377b090c795868828dd9e1
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-13 15:37:06 +1000

Reshaped the meaning of isForceUpdate.

CvSelectionGroup::readyToMove now considers isForceUpdate to be
equivalent to being awake with no missions queued.

Disabled CvSelectionGroup::doForceUpdate. Inlined setForceUpdate and
isForceUpdate.

'force update' no longer wakes sleeping units. The AI's sleeping units
are also not woken at the start of the turn. doForceUpdate is now simply
used to signal that the AI update function for each group should run at
least once per turn, regardless of whether the unit is awake or not.

AI_cancelGroupAttack and clearMissionQueue are now called explicitly
without being directly associated with force update.

With these changes, the AI can now use MISSIONS_FORTIFY if it wants.

commit a313cd6c46799b4e0bf9a99bcaea95034bfb2bde
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-08 21:06:28 +1000

Added UI option: Simple Selection, and changed the default behaviour.

The previous selection behaviour with the shift key is now called
'Simple [Shift] Unit Selection'. It can be enabled from the BUG options.
The non-'simple' behaviour is that when using shift to add units (not
remove), if the current selection mirrors a group, a shift will group
select, and form a group; otherwise, shift will not group-select nor
form a group.

commit aedc58c2899ccbb3851ccae32ced094727ea1053
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-07 18:46:20 +1000

Fixed a bug which made researched techs untradeable for 1 turn.

The bug was caused by a conflict between my new CvTeam::doTurn system
and an old rule from the original BtS code, which, as far as I can tell,
was only there to prevent bulbed techs from being immediately tradeable.

That bulbing rule has simply been removed.

commit aad77e264fac928db311a70bd14e0cf75c0b98f7
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-07 18:14:41 +1000

Some more behaviour changes & bug fixes with unit selection.

The selection with the shift key will now always affect only a single
target. This corrects some unpredictable (and unavoidable) behaviour.

Fixed a problem which sometimes caused units to not form a group when
they were suppose to.

commit d347386cd4e5efc6f4b12b9456983d8507166596
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-07 16:08:03 +1000

'Retire' and 'WorldBuilder' controls now always require confirmation.

Previously, doControl(CONTROL_RETIRE) would immediately execute the
retire functionalty with no confirmation. Selecting 'Retire' from the
main menu had an explicitly created confirmation popup.

In the new system, doControl(CONTROL_RETIRE) creates the confirmation
popup itself, and then the confirmation popup can call a new function
called CvGame::retire.

This means that retiring will require confirmation regardless of whether
the command came from the main menu or not and it will no require any
additional ad-hoc code. The 'WorldBuilder' command has be changed in a
similar way.

Also, the buttons in all confirmation popups now describe the action
that is to be confirmed. eg. if the action is 'retire', the buttons will
read "Retire" / "Cancel", rather than "Yes" / "No".

commit b2bada62d3b8267ceada60c2e69064d78029c38b
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-07 15:25:16 +1000

Fixed minor problems with nuke event messages and city capture sounds.

The city capture sound is now played when the city is actually captured,
rather than when the city capture popup is displayed. This prevents the
sound from repeating if the player chooses to examine the city.

Nuke messages (eg. player X's nuke exploded, or player Y intercepted X's
nuke) are now only shown to civs who player who have met the
civilzations involved in the event.

commit 68ba74b966351b8a221926d774800c6b569668cd

My recent changes to CvMap::updateCenterUnit were causing save games to
load without setting the center units correctly. To fix this,
CvPlot::setupGraphical now calls CvPlot::updateCenterUnit.

commit 1acc3942d453d47477cfd81c14fe6cb412ab3af8
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-07-05 12:50:00 +1000

Rewrote CvMap::updateCenterUnit to only update plots in a certain range.

Previously, CvMap::updateCenterUnit updated the center unit for every
plot on the map. This is quite an expensive thing to do; and it caused a
noticeable delay when selecting units on a big map. The new version only
updates the plots which could potentially be in movement range of the
units being selected. The range in determined using a new static
function: KmodPathFinder::MinimumStepCost.

commit a9ba4dda5626b13bf1c56b4fae2759cbfaddc241

Previously, units would be added the the group when selected, but not
removed from the group when deselected. This change fixes that
inconsistency.

commit 959bf1ce715cba8ee7098737b3a1adbdf3b6481a
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-06-30 14:36:15 +1000

CvTeam::doTurn is now always called at the start of the team's turn.

Previously it was called at the end of the team's turn in
non-simultaneous turns mode. But it occurs to me that if it is called at
the end of the turn, then counters such as the counter-espionage turn
counter will not work as expected. Consider, for example,
counter-espionage for 1 turn. If doTurn is called at the end of the
turn, that 1 turn of counter-espionage will end before the other
player's turn even starts.

commit e30c98604c17eef13ca65fa7744f2b521b34a70f

Made some minor adjustments to various AI functions.

commit 3d45a7f2a8489132400bbac8a49a7001b49a330c
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-06-27 23:07:12 +1000

Made several significant changes to the AI's war evaluations.

The plot values in CvTeamAI::AI_warSpoilsValue are now scaled by an
era-based factor so that they scale better into the late game. Also,
I've made some balance adjustments to the deny factor and the gain
factor; particularly for overseas total-war.

In CvTeamAI::AI_warCommitmentCost, the cost is now more strongly linked
to production. The relative production factor has been slightly
increased, and the overall commitment pool is now weighted more strongly
towards production than previously.
The 'iOurRelativeStrength' now includes the power of vassals.

Note: before these changes, late game wars were often being completely
blocked due to negative values.

commit 8efe75ac36463d4eb87f973b569cb0d13cf55810
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-06-24 22:18:32 +1000

Rearranged calls to CvTeam::doTurn to fix a balance problem.

CvTeam::doTurn is now called immediately after all players of the team
have finished their turn. (or, for simultaneous turn mode, immediately
before the first player's turn.) This corrects an problem which caused
counter-espionage missions to be unfairly more powerful for players
who's turns came first.

Some calls to CvPlayerAI::AI_updateAttitudeCache have been moved to
accommodate the change to doTurn.

commit bd52939528a602444fc2f569e257def61dd84fad
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-06-21 12:22:45 +1000

Changed the close-border attitude cache system.

The original close-border attitude cache system was prone to the same
OOS problems as the CAR mod cache system. So I have rewritten it using
the same, more robust, style as the new cache system.

ie. The cache is updated rigidly at the start of each turn rather than
being updated when it is first called upon.

commit 67ad94aa6b6c344f30cf053e72d96694178db695
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-06-21 13:01:42 +1000

commit 575791c4b1c2a55aa2092133f2790f6213d1fbad
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-06-21 10:21:54 +1000

Completely rewrote the attitude cache system.

Cache is now recalculated on the spot when something happens to
invalidate it.

CvPlayerAI::AI_invalidateAttitudeCache has been removed and repalced
with CvPlayerAI::AI_updateAttitudeCache.

The original system (from the 'CAR' mod) was hideously prone to OOS
bugs. I'd fixed many of the bugs in the past, but there were still more,
and it was extremely difficult to write robust code using that system.
(Here a couple of lingering bug examples from the old system.
CvTeam::declareWar didn't clear cache for the friends of the war target.
CvTeam::signDefensivePact didn't clear cache for the rivals of each
team. The crux of the problem is that in the CAR system, it was the
programmers responsibility to know exactly how and when attitude could
be affected in any way, and then clear the cache when it was affected.
If the programmer overlooked anything, it could cause OOS.)

The new system is more rigid in when the cache is recalculated; and so
similar 'bugs' will now no longer result in OOS. At worst they will
result in out-of-date attitude ratings. In fact, this is such a minor
effect that I've deliberately removed the cache update from a few places
where I think it is unimportant.

commit 3b2066831b96ac5eeb5efb6f9391afc2dc33d0bd
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-06-14 17:55:59 +1000

First draft of my rewrite of the AI war evaluation routines.

Added new functions: CvTeamAI::AI_warSpoilsValue, AI_warCommitmentCost,
and AI_warDiplomacyCost. These functions are used to evaluate various
aspects of war. Spoils value is an estimate of what could be gained by
winning the war. Commitment cost is an estimate of how much resources
would need to be devoted toward the war. Diplomacy cost is an estimate
of how much a DoW would hurt the player's diplomatic standings.

CvTeamAI::AI_startWarVal now relies solely on these 3 new functions.
CvTeamAI::AI_endWarVal is currently unchanged, but I will probably later
rework it to use the new functions as well.

commit 9a6f57339e03f8c39e466aa66505c755d36b0114
Author: karadoc <hidden>

CvTeam::announceTechToPlayers now uses the single player sound rather than the MP sound. (ie. the quote rather than "you have discovered X")

commit 0ba3b7904ed2bc55cc891dd6b43a7d0bc4e899ee
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-05-15 01:19:48 +1000

Rewrote some sections related to the loading of game text xml.
The format of the xml is now more intuitive and less rigid. In particular, the language names used to tag the xml values are now what determines the text for any given language. The order of the languages is no longer important (except in CIV4GameText_Misc1.xml, which is used to determine the correct tag names). If there is a missing language, the first text will be used (usually english). The mod can include languages from all localizations of Civ4, not just the version I happen to be using.

commit c127ada99f54ac4bffdcfdb44986d9fd3c5e083a

Changed mercantilism upkeep from 'medium' to 'low'. [This civic is often worse than decentralization. I think it deserves to be a bit stronger.]

commit 311a46576f5df1eb8ae43a4b82b91a4d1c020666
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-05-10 23:43:41 +1000

Changed groupCycleDistance so that it checks the actual order of units on the plot rather than using 'isBeforeUnitCycle' to decide which unit should come first.
The reason for this is that units on the plot can get out of order in some situations; and when this happens, it is better if the group cycle order matches the order on the plot rather than the ideal order.

commit 27f100e8ffd9c9d3b1c709729e731777608efdfb

commit 66a4f53a989a8beb2ccb883e3dc8fb6da24e4ea2
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-05-10 12:43:52 +1000

Combat animations are now choreographed to roughly correspond to the events in the combat log. As a result, the battles will be a bit more varied and less predictable.
(Rewrote CvUnit::planBattle; signficantly changed CvUnit::updateCombat and CvUnit::resolveCombat. The underlying game mechanics have not been changed.)

commit 58beb5a9421bda5971d10bd71bb278c91fb9c72a
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-05-09 10:53:17 +1000

Rewrote the conditions for CvSeletionGroup::canStartMission and the conditions for continueing a mission so that they are consistent with one another and so that they are less restrictive about which missions can be executed. For example, fortify, sentry, and heal can now be executed by units without any movement points. Bombard and airstrike can be executed by groups which include units that can't move, and so on.
autoMission will no longer call continueMission when the group is not ready to continue. (this prevents some unnessary unit cycling.)

commit ed87b233f0674d32a7cad276ec74e80f8dd30d15
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-05-09 10:52:44 +1000

Units will once again wake up when forming a group. This prevents an exploit which could allow players to bypass the movement check on air patrol and sea patrol missions.
The pillage command will now cause the whole group to pillage rather than just one unit. (This is the original functionality. It was changed by BBAI.)
The game will now cycle selection groups when an order is issued to a group which is not ready to move; just as it cycles when the order is to a group which can move.
Fixed a problem with Rapid Unit Cycling which caused it not to cycle for some particular missions, including air patrol and sea patrol.
Fixed a problem with the original unit cycling which caused the game to cycle when units form a group in certain situations.

commit 0315c6343ebb3bada6fec8e727241a0d8d09a8cc
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-03-23 11:10:34 +1100

Added a new argument, bAssumeVisible, to CvUnit::canMoveInto and its sister functions.
 - Previously, canMoveInto checked plot visibility for human players but not for AI players. Now it will check visibility iff !bAssumeVisible.
Added a new movement flag, MOVE_ASSUME_VISIBLE. This flag is set when the next step towards the target plot would take us backwards. When it is set, bAssumeVisible is used. (Note: bAssumeVisible is always used for AI players regardless of this flag.)
These changes fix the problem of human units forgetting that the path to their destination is blocked by an enemy unit as they lose sight of the enemy unit.

Deleted some obsolete code.

When ordered to attack an adjacent plot, human controlled groups will now move their directly instead of trying to find the shortest path. (Previously, they would sometimes want to step off their origin plot so that they could enter the target plot by road, and thus save some movement points. commit 7f52c723d2580572b6c53a04988f6f7d135bf36d
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-03-12 23:00:52 +1100

Added new function, CvPlayer::doesImprovementConnectBonus, which is meant to be a definitive indicator of whether or not a particular improvement will give the player access the bonus.
Cleaned up inconsistent and incorrect code throughout many parts of the code.
(Previously, there was some confusion in the code as to how such a check should be done. It turns out that there were _lots_ of bugs due to "isImprovementBonusTrade" being used on its own to decide if a bonus would be connected. The bugs affected a broad range of functions in the AI and in the UI; including the count of "unimproved bonuses" and "improvable bonuses", and worker build recommendations, and pillage evaluation, and so on.)

commit 2711e7fee3bc98f19cc19320427de63e5b6fbfd9
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-03-11 14:28:20 +1100

AI_civicValue now only counts the most valued potential corporation when speculating, rather than all potential corporations. Also, if when player does not have the prereqs for the civic being evaluated, then the prereqs are not checked for corporation speculation. (ie. the corps are valued regardless of whether the prereqs are met.)
Resource bonuses produced by corps are now valued in AI_civicValue.
Added new option to CvPlayerAI::AI_bonusValue, bAssumeEnabled. if bAssumeEnabled == false, and the team does not have the tech to enable or reveal the resource, the the resource is devalued.
Removed the default value for iChange in AI_bonusValue. There was some confusion as to whether the default was 1 or 0, and it was misused in many parts of the code, so I've removed the default and explicitly specified the parameter in all of the function calls.

commit 550a29006684f1cd1bc4171d28d689698bf3a4d0
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-03-02 15:24:00 +1100

MISSION_SKIP will now automatically unload units from transports if the mission comes with a not-trival MISSIONAI. (This is to prevent units from trying to guard cities while still in their transport.)
Disabled a bunch of BBAI code in continueMission which did some ad-hoc hackery for picking up strandard units. My new AI_handleStranded function doesn't require the old BBAI code, and the old code allowed the AI the cheat by allowing units to step onto a boat without needing movement points.

Moved the AI_load condition in AI_attackCityMove to be above the group condition, so that units don't leave their transports to group with their old stack!
In all of the naval Move functions: changed the city-under-threat condition to not include the case where there is no defence and not attack. (This is to allow boats to actually leave from undefended cities.)
CvUnitAI::AI_load, will now only split the group trying to load if the target transport is more than 1 turn away.

Adjusted pathCost to discourage units from ending their turn on tiles that they cannot fight on. (eg. boats in cities)
Added a new function, CvSelectionGroup::cargoSpaceAvailable. (currently unused)
Strengthed the condition for the AI automatically give a skip order to units on transports.

commit 2df55370363c2b2ee8347ea59d620ce5e66b8c2b
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2012-02-23 14:07:03 +1100

The AI is now less likely to start building things which will be obsoleted by their current research.
Changed the way the AI weights unit value based on how many units are built. Hopefully this will stop the AI from building vastly inferior units just because it doesn't have any yet.
The AI is now less likely to be motivated to use its espionage slider by dislike.
AI_buildingValue will no longer count how many cities are building the building unless it needs to know. (This will make it slightly faster.)
Fixed a problem which could cause an infinite loop in certain mal-thought-out mods. (ie. if two buildings have each other as prereqs, previously it would cause an infinite loop in the AI. Now it does not.)

Amphibious landings can now be executed even if the group isn't ready to move. (eg. if you have a transport on the coast, with no moves left, ording it to move onto the land will now cause it to unload its cargo.)
Automated units will wait until the "automoves" phase before moving, rather than doing it as soon as it is their turn. (This will require some testing to make sure I haven't messed something up.)

commit 93129578f82fab64eaf0ca2ca44bb2fd6210aaed
Author: karadoc <hidden>
Date:   2011-10-14 19:34:00 +1100

Rewrote the pathCost function used by the pathfinding. This fixes several pathfinding problems & bugs. Most notably, the new code allows the AI to choose a path with involves fighting through enemy units that are in the way; the new code does not take into account movement costs or defensive bonuses for tiles that have not been revealed (this makes the AI cheat less, but it also removes an exploit which allowed human players to determin the positions of islands, roads, etc.).
Restructured various parts of the code.
Blocked some more python callbacks.
Gave the AI some basic ability to target poorly defended cities.
Adjusted the threshold for the AI skipping the bombard stage of city attacks.
Fixed a potential future bug in the change which makes it possible to capture defenseless units after attacking... the potential problem was that the code was using "getNumVisibleEnemyDefenders", when it fact it should include all kinds of enemy defenders. (As it happens, getNumVisibleEnemyDefenders does not currently check that the defenders can actually in sight...)
